name: Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop
name_original: Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana - Ruta ciruclar
description: This easy 3.5-mile (5.7 km) circular hiking route around Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana offers continuous lake views and excellent birdwatching.
region_intro: Hiking around Ivars D'Urgell offers routes through the Pla d'Urgell region, characterized by significant natural wetlands and agricultural landscapes. The area's primary feature is the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana, Catalonia's largest inland lake, which provides a rich biodiversity. Trails also lead to historical sites like the Pilar de Almenara Tower, offering panoramic views of the surrounding plains. The terrain is generally flat, with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for various activity levels. Best hiking trails around Ivars D'Urgell The most popular hiking route is Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana, offering opportunities for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pilar de Almenara - Circular Route, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail leads to the historic Pilar de Almenara Tower, providing panoramic views of the Urgell plain. Local hikers also love the Third Canal of Urgell Path – Second Bridge loop from Mollerussa, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Ivars D'Urgell is defined by wetland paths, agricultural fields, and historical viewpoints, with options for easy and moderate routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily focusing on flat terrain. The routes in Ivars D'Urgell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Ivars D'Urgell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Ivars D'Urgell?
A: The hiking trails in Ivars D'Urgell are generally flat, characterized by wetland paths around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana and agricultural fields. While most routes have minimal elevation changes, some, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938255744/pilar-de-almenara-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, offer slight inclines leading to viewpoints.

Q: Are there many easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Ivars D'Urgell?
A: Yes, Ivars D'Urgell is an excellent destination for beginners. There are over 18 easy routes available, making up more than half of the total trails. These routes typically feature flat terrain and well-maintained paths, perfect for leisurely walks.

Q: What are the main natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in the area?
A: The primary natural feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana&lt;/a&gt;, Catalonia's largest inland lake, known for its rich biodiversity and aquatic birds. You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara Tower&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of the Urgell plain, and the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of San Vicente de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular walking routes around Ivars D'Urgell?
A: Yes, circular routes are a highlight of the area. The most prominent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938197658/ivars-and-vila-sana-pond-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938255744/pilar-de-almenara-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, offering historical sights and views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ivars D'Urgell?
A: The region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, especially around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana for birdwatching. Summer can be warm, but the flat terrain and proximity to the lake make it manageable, particularly in the mornings or late afternoons. Winter provides a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Ivars D'Urgell suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails around Ivars D'Urgell are very family-friendly due to their generally flat and easy terrain. The circular path around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana, for instance, features boardwalks, rest areas, and observation points, making it engaging for children. There are 18 easy routes in total, perfect for family outings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ivars D'Urgell?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Ivars D'Urgell are suitable for dogs, especially the open paths around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana and through agricultural landscapes. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural fields, and to respect local regulations regarding pets.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trailheads in Ivars D'Urgell?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads, particularly around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana. For example, the visitor and interpretation center at Cal Sinén, which serves as a starting point for the lake's circular path, typically offers parking facilities. Specific route details on komoot often include information about parking at the trailhead.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ivars D'Urgell?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the tranquil atmosphere, the abundant birdwatching opportunities around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana, and the well-maintained, accessible paths that cater to all ability levels.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or picnic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, particularly around the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana. The circular route includes rest areas with tables and benches, as well as designated picnic zones, making it convenient for longer outings. While specific cafes directly on trails might be limited, the nearby towns like Ivars D'Urgell and Mollerussa offer various dining options.

Q: How long do the hiking trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration of hikes in Ivars D'Urgell varies depending on the route and your pace. Many easy trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938197658/ivars-and-vila-sana-pond-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop&lt;/a&gt; (5.7 km), can be completed in under 1.5 hours. Longer moderate routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond loop from Aparcament Estany d'Ivars i Vilasana Oest&lt;/a&gt; (10.9 km), might take closer to 2.5-3 hours.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options if I prefer solitude?
A: While the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana is a popular spot, the region offers 33 different routes, including those through agricultural landscapes. Exploring trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17547430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Third Canal of Urgell Path – Second Bridge loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through fields, might offer a quieter experience, especially during off-peak hours or seasons.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana Circular Route?
faqs.answer: You can find parking available at the Cal Sinén Visitor Center, which also serves as an information and interpretation hub for the lake. It's a convenient starting point for the route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is consistently described as easy in terms of both technical and physical difficulty. Its flat, well-defined path makes it suitable for beginners, families, and even those with baby strollers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route follows a generally flat and even path around the lake. You'll encounter a mix of landscapes, including areas with tamarisks, margins of cultivated fields, and a small forest (Bosc de Ca l'Aragonès). There are also sections with walkways and jetties.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana is an 'oasis of life' rich in flora and fauna. It's an important habitat for over 210 different species of aquatic birds, making it an excellent spot for birdwatching. You'll find specific observation hides and viewpoints along the route to enhance your chances of spotting them.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attraction is the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond itself, offering continuous views. You'll also pass the Termenal Viewpoint of the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond and a Birdwatching Hide at Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana. Information panels are placed throughout, detailing local flora, fauna, and the lake's history.

faqs.question: Is the Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana Circular Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs within the Ivars and Vila-Sana Pond Natural Space should be checked locally upon arrival, especially concerning protected wildlife areas. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local ecosystem.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round due to its easy nature. However, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, making them ideal for birdwatching and enjoying the restored wetland's beauty. Summer can be warm, so consider early mornings or late afternoons.

faqs.question: Are there any entrance fees or permits required for the Ivars and Vila-Sana Pond Natural Space?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or permits required to access the Ivars and Vila-Sana Pond Natural Space or to hike this circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route fully encompasses the 'Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana' trail and also covers a significant portion of the 'Camí de l'Estany' trail.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms or picnic areas available?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cal Sinén Visitor Center, located at the start of the route, provides restrooms and picnic areas. It's a great spot to relax before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What makes the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana unique?
faqs.answer: The Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana is unique for its remarkable history of ecological recovery. Once drained for agriculture, it underwent a significant restoration project starting in 2004, leading to a 'true explosion of life' as the natural space recovered. It's now the largest inland lake in Catalonia and a prime example of successful wetland restoration in Europe.
